response: The coach who was replaced in 1996 as the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues was **Gregg Popovich**. He took over as the head coach of the San Antonio Spurs in 1996, and prior to that, he was the head coach of the Phoenix Suns. Popovich became the longest tenured active coach in both the NBA and all US major sports leagues, and he was replaced in 1996 by **Bob Hill**, the head coach of the San Antonio Spurs.
